vocabulary.contextTranslation: 嗯，几千年来，女性似乎一直在平衡全职工作和母亲身份

vocabulary.definition: n. 名声, 宣传, 公开场合；[经] 宣传, 广告
vocabulary.context: It's true that women, across the world, often earn little credit or publicity for their contributions on farms.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 确实，世界各地的妇女往往因其对农场的贡献而获得很少的荣誉或宣传

vocabulary.definition: n. 生存, 生活；[医] 生存
vocabulary.context: And by looking at subsistence farming communities today, she says, you can speculate about what types of tasks ancient women were doing: "Hoeing, planting, harvesting, chopping wood, grinding grain and getting water - and all this while raising small children."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她说，通过观察今天的自给农业社区，你可以推测古代妇女正在做哪些类型的任务："锄地、种植、收割、砍柴、磨谷物和取水--所有这些都是在抚养小孩的同时进行的。"

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 深思, 推测, 投机；[经] 投机
vocabulary.context: And by looking at subsistence farming communities today, she says, you can speculate about what types of tasks ancient women were doing: "Hoeing, planting, harvesting, chopping wood, grinding grain and getting water - and all this while raising small children."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她说，通过观察今天的自给农业社区，你可以推测古代妇女正在做哪些类型的任务："锄地、种植、收割、砍柴、磨谷物和取水--所有这些都是在抚养小孩的同时进行的。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 兴奋剂, 补品；adj. 热忱的, 诚恳的, 有兴奋作用的
vocabulary.context: ...crank, and the machine inquired as to our health, asked how we liked the phonograph, informed us that it was very well, and bid us a cordial good night." Interest was great, and the invention was reported in several New York newspapers, and later in other American newspapers and magazines.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...曲柄，机器询问我们的健康状况，询问我们是否喜欢留声机，告诉我们它很好，并亲切地向我们道了晚安。"人们的兴趣很浓厚，纽约的几家报纸以及后来的其他美国报纸和杂志都报道了这项发明

vocabulary.definition: n. 回想, 回忆, 怀旧, 话旧

vocabulary.context: The value of a humanoid over other designs is this ability to use the same workspace and tools - not only does this improve efficiency in the types of tools, but also removes the need for specialized robotic connectors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与其他设计相比，人形机器人的价值在于能够使用相同的工作空间和工具 - 这不仅提高了工具类型的效率，而且消除了对专门机器人连接器的需求

vocabulary.definition: adj. 汽车的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在 Robonaut、Robonaut 2 或 R2 的当前迭代中，NASA 和通用汽车正在 Oceaneering Space Systems 工程师的协助下合作，加速开发用于汽车和航空航天工业的下一代机器人和相关技术

vocabulary.context: Abigail Adams was mocked for being too influential over her husband, Dolley Madison was said to be too fancy, because she wore expensive gowns and jewelry, and Martha Washington felt that the strain of public life made her feel like a prisoner.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿比盖尔·亚当斯因对丈夫影响力过大而被嘲笑，多莉·麦迪逊被认为过于花哨，因为她穿着昂贵的礼服和珠宝，而玛莎·华盛顿则觉得公共生活的压力让她感觉自己像个囚犯

vocabulary.definition: adj. 多国的, 跨国公司的；n. 跨国公司
vocabulary.context: Pollution can be as simple as a dead animal dumped into the river that supplies a village, or as complex as large multinational corporations building factories that pollute water sources.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 污染可以像将死去的动物倾倒到为村庄供水的河流中一样简单，也可以像大型跨国公司建造污染水源的工厂一样复杂

vocabulary.definition: n. 铬；[化] 铬Cr
vocabulary.context: In Ranipet, India, leather tanning wastes contaminate groundwater with the chemical hexavalent chromium, leading to water that residents say stings like an insect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在印度的拉尼佩特，皮革鞣制废物用化学物质六价铬污染了地下水，导致居民称水像昆虫一样刺痛

vocabulary.definition: n. 黄褐色, 鞣料, 马戏团；adj. 黄褐色的, 鞣皮的；v.i. 晒成棕褐色；v.t. 把...晒黑, 鞣(革), 狠狠鞭打
vocabulary.context: In Ranipet, India, leather tanning wastes contaminate groundwater with the chemical hexavalent chromium, leading to water that residents say stings like an insect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在印度的拉尼佩特，皮革鞣制废物用化学物质六价铬污染了地下水，导致居民称水像昆虫一样刺痛

vocabulary.definition: n. 锅柄, 锅柄状地区, 狭长土地；v. 街头行乞, 讨钱
vocabulary.context: In the United States, the National Drought Mitigation Center finds only the panhandle of Oklahoma and northern Georgia experiencing 'extreme drought'.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在美国，国家干旱缓解中心发现，只有俄克拉荷马州和乔治亚州北部的狭长地带经历了"极度干旱"

vocabulary.definition: n. 夸张, 夸大的事物, 夸大之词
vocabulary.context: That's not an exaggeration or a metaphor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这并不是夸张或比喻

vocabulary.definition: n. 中提琴
vocabulary.context: Viola Liuzzo was a high school dropout and mother of five who became involved in the Civil Rights Movement when she returned to school.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 维奥拉·利乌佐 (Viola Liuzzo) 是一名高中辍学者，也是五个孩子的母亲，回到学校后参与了民权运动

vocabulary.definition: v. 汽车合用组织( car pool的第三人称单数 )
vocabulary.context: They arranged carpools and had bake sales to raise money for alternative transportation for those people who normally took the bus.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们安排拼车并举办烘焙义卖活动，为那些通常乘坐公共汽车的人筹集替代交通的资金

vocabulary.definition: n. 性别歧视, 歧视女性
vocabulary.context: However, in spite of the importance of women in the Civil Rights Movement, they had to deal with sexism and discrimination within the movement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，尽管妇女在民权运动中很重要，但她们必须应对运动中的性别歧视和歧视

vocabulary.definition: n. 豹, 黑豹, 美洲豹
vocabulary.context: Women were the majority of members in many local chapters of civil rights organizations like the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP) and the Black Panther Party.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在全国有色人种协进会 (NAACP) 和黑豹党等民权组织的许多地方分会中，女性成员占大多数

vocabulary.definition: n. 授权; 许可
vocabulary.context: The Black Power Movement started in 1966 as a movement that called for Black empowerment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 黑人权力运动始于 1966 年，是一场呼吁黑人赋权的运动

vocabulary.definition: n. 地基, 基础, 根据
vocabulary.context: Septima Clark was an activist whose work laid the groundwork for the Civil Rights Movement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 塞普蒂玛·克拉克 (Septima Clark) 是一位活动家，她的工作为民权运动奠定了基础

vocabulary.definition: adv. 性别地；两性之间地
vocabulary.context: Even within the Civil Rights Movement, they were often denied positions of leadership, overshadowed by men, and sexually harassed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 即使在民权运动中，她们也经常被剥夺领导职位，被男性掩盖，并受到性骚扰

vocabulary.definition: n. 承认, 答谢, 认可, 谢礼, 收到的通知, 收到的回音, 谢意；[计] 肯定, 确认认可
vocabulary.context: However, these women did not allow discrimination to prevent them from being part of the fight for racial justice, seeking leadership positions, and struggling within the movement for equal treatment and acknowledgment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，这些妇女不允许歧视阻止她们参与争取种族正义的斗争、寻求领导职位以及在争取平等待遇和承认的运动中进行斗争

vocabulary.definition: n. 泛非主义, 泛非运动
vocabulary.context: He embraced socialism and pan-Africanism and converted to Sunni Islam.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他拥护社会主义和泛非主义，并皈依逊尼派伊斯兰教

vocabulary.definition: n. 火焰炸弹(燃烧弹和凝固汽油弹的总称)
vocabulary.context: His home was firebombed with his four daughters inside, but luckily they were able to escape unharmed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的家遭到燃烧弹袭击，他的四个女儿都在里面，但幸运的是她们能够安全逃脱

vocabulary.definition: n. 挖墓者, 埋葬虫
vocabulary.context: 1500 people attended his funeral, and his friends took the shovels away from gravediggers to honor him by burying him themselves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1500人参加了他的葬礼，他的朋友们从掘墓人手中拿走了铲子，亲自埋葬了他，以示对他的敬意

vocabulary.definition: n. 夜生活
vocabulary.context: Through these jobs and nightlife on the streets, he was introduced to a world of crime.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过这些工作和街头的夜生活，他进入了一个犯罪世界

vocabulary.definition: adv. 忠诚地, 忠义地, 诚实地
vocabulary.context: Malcolm had loyally followed all of Elijah Muhammad's teachings, and felt disappointed and betrayed by Elijah Muhammad's actions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 马尔科姆忠实地遵循了以利亚·穆罕默德的所有教义，并对以利亚·穆罕默德的行为感到失望和背叛

vocabulary.definition: n. 殖民主义
vocabulary.context: He met a lot of different people on his trip and learned a lot about Islam and the fight against colonialism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他在旅途中遇到了很多不同的人，了解了很多关于伊斯兰教和反对殖民主义的知识

vocabulary.definition: adj. 魅力, 能吸引大众的非凡能力, 神授的能力, 领袖人物感人的超凡魅力
vocabulary.context: He was, by then, already a charismatic person and very good at speaking to people.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那时他已经是一个有魅力的人，非常善于与人交谈

vocabulary.definition: adj. 精神上的, 神圣的, 崇高的, 高尚的, 鬼的, 招魂术的；n. 有关教会的事
vocabulary.context: His brother, Reginald, regularly visited and told him about a religious group called the Nation of Islam (NOI), headed by the spiritual leader, Elijah Muhammad.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的兄弟雷金纳德经常拜访他，并向他介绍了一个名为"伊斯兰民族"（NOI）的宗教团体，该团体由精神领袖伊利亚·穆罕默德领导

vocabulary.definition: n. 绑架；诱拐
vocabulary.context: According to a CNN news report, Boko Haram's leader at the time, Abubakar Shekau, admitted to the kidnappings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据CNN新闻报道，博科圣地时任领导人阿布巴卡尔·谢考承认了绑架事件

vocabulary.definition: n. (=harem)(伊斯兰教徒)女眷居住的内室,闺房, (伊斯兰教徒的)女眷,共配一雄的一群雌性动物
vocabulary.context: The attack brought worldwide attention to Boko Haram, a terrorist group that controls "BBOG3MAYNYC_DSC_0291" by Michael northeast Nigeria.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这次袭击引起了全世界对博科圣地的关注，这是一个由迈克尔控制尼日利亚东北部"BBOG3MAYNYC_DSC_0291"的恐怖组织

vocabulary.definition: n. 方言, 同源语
vocabulary.context: The name Boko Haram is a mix of Arabic and the Nigerian Hausa dialect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 博科圣地这个名字是阿拉伯语和尼日利亚豪萨方言的混合体

vocabulary.definition: n. 人质, 抵押品；[经] 人质, 抵押品
vocabulary.context: In 2015, the new Nigerian President, Muhammadu Buhari, promised to rescue the hostages from Boko Haram.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2015年，尼日利亚新任总统穆罕默杜·布哈里承诺从博科圣地手中解救人质

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使困窘, 使局促不安, 阻碍
vocabulary.context: They stated that it was staged in an attempt to embarrass the government.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们表示，这是为了让政府难堪而策划的

vocabulary.definition: n. 骚动, 喧嚣
vocabulary.context: International uproar and support was generated around the rallying cry "bring back our girls" - a quote taken from a speech that a former government minister, Obiageli Ezekwesili, made on TV.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "带回我们的女孩"这一口号引起了国际社会的轩然大波和支持--这句话摘自前政府部长奥比亚杰利·埃泽奎西里 (Obiageli Ezekwesili) 在电视上发表的演讲

vocabulary.definition: n. 净化, 清除, 泻药；v. (使)净化, 清除, (使)通便；[计] 服务器文件删除实用程序
vocabulary.context: It translates to "western education is forbidden." Boko Haram is fighting to turn Nigeria into an Islamic state and purge the country of anything they consider to be of Western influence, such as schools and churches.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 翻译过来就是"禁止西方教育"。博科圣地正在努力将尼日利亚变成一个伊斯兰国家，并清除该国任何他们认为受西方影响的东西，例如学校和教堂

vocabulary.definition: n. 升起, 起立, 上山坡, 出现；v.i. 上升, 起义, 出现
vocabulary.context: On the heels of a Boko Haram uprising, in 2009, Yusuf was captured and killed by the Nigerian military.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2009年博科圣地起义之后，优素福被尼日利亚军方抓获并杀害

vocabulary.definition: adj. 在前面的; 坦率的; 预付的
vocabulary.context: Vegetables are actually cheaper per calorie than fast food, but the up-front costs are higher.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 蔬菜的每卡路里热量实际上比快餐便宜，但前期成本更高

vocabulary.definition: n. 支付能力；负担能力；可购性
vocabulary.context: For these reasons, the USDA also takes affordability of fresh food into account when determining whether an area is a food desert.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 出于这些原因，美国农业部在确定一个地区是否为食物荒漠时也会考虑新鲜食品的承受能力

vocabulary.definition: adj. 服务不周到的,服务水平低下的
vocabulary.context: Expanding supermarket access - perhaps one of the most obvious solutions is to give supermarket chains more incentives to build branches in low-income, underserved neighborhoods.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 扩大超市准入--也许最明显的解决方案之一是给予连锁超市更多激励，在低收入、服务不足的社区建立分支机构

vocabulary.definition: n. 卡路里（热量单位）
vocabulary.context: Vegetables are actually cheaper per calorie than fast food, but the up-front costs are higher.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 蔬菜的每卡路里热量实际上比快餐便宜，但前期成本更高

vocabulary.definition: n. 卡路里（热量单位, calorie的复数）
vocabulary.context: Fast food and processed foods have a lot more calories per meal than fresh, whole foods.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 快餐和加工食品每餐比新鲜的天然食品含有更多的热量

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不健康的
vocabulary.context: While the solutions to food deserts around the country have been highly innovative in the last several years, it is important to note that food deserts are not the main factor in unhealthy eating habits and obesity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然过去几年全国各地针对食物荒漠的解决方案极具创新性，但值得注意的是，食物荒漠并不是不健康饮食习惯和肥胖的主要因素

vocabulary.definition: n. 糖尿病, 多尿症；[医] 糖尿病; 多尿症
vocabulary.context: Those who do not have access to plant-based, nutrient-rich foods are much more likely to suffer from obesity, heart disease, and type-2 diabetes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那些无法获得营养丰富的植物性食物的人更有可能患上肥胖、心脏病和 2 型糖尿病

vocabulary.definition: adv. 戏剧地, 引人注目地, 突然地
vocabulary.context: Since 1989, the price of fresh foods has increased dramatically, while processed foods have only become more affordable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自1989年以来，新鲜食品的价格急剧上涨，而加工食品却变得更加实惠

vocabulary.definition: n. 硬化症, 硬化, 僵化；[医] 硬化
vocabulary.context: Worse news came when he was diagnosed with motor neuron disease, also known as amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), and given only two years to live.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 更糟糕的消息传来，他被诊断出患有运动神经元疾病，也称为肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS），并且只剩下两年的生命

vocabulary.definition: n. 榜样；崇拜对象
vocabulary.context: However, he was also well-known throughout the world as a role-model for people with disabilities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，他也作为残疾人的榜样而闻名于世

vocabulary.definition: adj. 预言性的, 成为前兆的
vocabulary.context: As his disease progressed, he used more and more advanced technology, including predictive software and computers that sensed his cheek movements.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着病情的进展，他使用了越来越多的先进技术，包括预测软件和感知他脸颊运动的计算机

vocabulary.definition: n. 宇宙哲学, 宇宙论；[电] 宇宙论
vocabulary.context: Stephen Hawking was one of the most famous theoretical physicists of our time, and not just for his revolutionary work in physics and cosmology.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 斯蒂芬·霍金是我们这个时代最著名的理论物理学家之一，不仅仅是因为他在物理学和宇宙学方面的革命性工作

vocabulary.definition: n. 有关系, 相关性, 相对性；[化] 相对性
vocabulary.context: Over his lifetime, his contributions to scientific knowledge of relativity, black holes, quantum mechanics, and the universe's beginning were immense.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他的一生中，他对相对论、黑洞、量子力学和宇宙起源等科学知识的贡献是巨大的

vocabulary.definition: n. 量, 量子；[计] 量子
vocabulary.context: Over his lifetime, his contributions to scientific knowledge of relativity, black holes, quantum mechanics, and the universe's beginning were immense.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他的一生中，他对相对论、黑洞、量子力学和宇宙起源等科学知识的贡献是巨大的

vocabulary.definition: n. 赌注, 赌博, 赌物；v.t. 下赌注, 向...保证；v.i. 打赌
vocabulary.context: Hawking frequently made wagers with other scientists about whose theory would eventually be proven correct; he often won, but not always!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 霍金经常与其他科学家打赌，看谁的理论最终会被证明是正确的。他经常获胜，但并非总是如此！

vocabulary.definition: n. 侧部, 支线, 边音；adj. 侧面的, 旁边的
vocabulary.context: Worse news came when he was diagnosed with motor neuron disease, also known as amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), and given only two years to live.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 更糟糕的消息传来，他被诊断出患有运动神经元疾病，也称为肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS），并且只剩下两年的生命

vocabulary.definition: adj. 理论的, 理论上的, 假设的, 推理的；[计] 理论的
vocabulary.context: Stephen Hawking was one of the most famous theoretical physicists of our time, and not just for his revolutionary work in physics and cosmology.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 斯蒂芬·霍金是我们这个时代最著名的理论物理学家之一，不仅仅是因为他在物理学和宇宙学方面的革命性工作

vocabulary.definition: n. 超声；[计] 超声
vocabulary.context: The beam consists of ultrasound waves, which humans cannot hear, but which can emit audible tones as they interact with air.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 光束由超声波组成，人类听不到，但在与空气相互作用时可以发出可听的音调

vocabulary.definition: n. 商标；标志
vocabulary.context: He trademarked the technology Audio Spotlight and started Holosonics, Inc., in Watertown, Mass., in 1999.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他为 Audio Spotlight 技术注册了商标，并于 1999 年在马萨诸塞州沃特敦创办了 Holosonics, Inc.

vocabulary.definition: adj. 方向的
vocabulary.context: Whereas a loudspeaker broadcasts sound in all directions, the way a lightbulb radiates light, a directional speaker shines a beam of waves akin to a spotlight.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 扬声器向各个方向传播声音，就像灯泡辐射光一样，而定向扬声器则像聚光灯一样发出光束

vocabulary.definition: n. <美>汽车制造商
vocabulary.context: Department stores have tried the arrangement for retail displays, and automakers are experimenting with them so passengers can hear only their own music or movies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 百货公司已经尝试过零售展示的安排，汽车制造商也在进行试验，以便乘客只能听到自己的音乐或电影

vocabulary.definition: n. 蜗；[医] (耳)蜗
vocabulary.context: But researchers have applied ultrasound up to 200,000 Hz to the skulls of volunteers, some of whom report "hearing" sounds; the skull may be distorting vibrations that reach the cochlea.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但研究人员已将高达 200,000 Hz 的超声波应用于志愿者的头骨，其中一些人报告"听到"声音；头骨可能会扭曲到达耳蜗的振动

vocabulary.definition: n. 赫, 赫兹；[计] 赫兹
vocabulary.context: The ultrasound speakers emit frequencies from 40,000 to 80,000 cycles a second, or hertz (Hz).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 超声波扬声器发出每秒 40,000 至 80,000 个周期或赫兹 (Hz) 的频率

vocabulary.definition: adv. 算术地
vocabulary.context: By describing these interactions mathematically, engineers can coax a beam to exude voice, music, or any other sound.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过以数学方式描述这些相互作用，工程师可以诱导光束发出声音、音乐或任何其他声音

vocabulary.definition: n. 扩大的人(物), 放大器, 扩音器；[计] 放大器
vocabulary.context: He then designed an amplifier, electronics and speakers to produce ultrasound "that is clean enough to generate clean audio," Pompei says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，他设计了一个放大器、电子设备和扬声器来产生"足够干净的超声波，可以产生干净的音频，"庞贝说

vocabulary.definition: n. 催眠状态, 催眠术；[医] 催眠, 催眠状态
vocabulary.context: Franz Anton Mesmer, a German doctor, used hypnosis to separate his alter ego.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 德国医生弗朗茨·安东·梅斯梅尔（Franz Anton Mesmer）使用催眠术来分离他的另一个自我

vocabulary.definition: adj. 原型的
vocabulary.context: The archetypal comic book hero, Superman, takes on the identity of the "mild-mannered" newspaper reporter Clark Kent in order to live among the citizens of Metropolis without making people suspicious.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 漫画英雄原型超人为了不引起人们的怀疑，以"温和"的报社记者克拉克·肯特的身份生活在大都会的市民中

vocabulary.definition: adj. 清楚的, 好看的, 轮廓鲜明的
vocabulary.context: Sometimes, he is Harvey Dent, the clean-cut lawyer of Gotham City; other times, he is Two-Face, a ruthless monster who uses his dual-faced coin to determine who lives and dies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有时，他是哈维·登特，哥谭市干净利落的律师；有时，他是双面人，一个无情的怪物，用他的双面硬币来决定谁的生死

vocabulary.definition: n. 精神病患者, 精神变态者；[医] 精神变态者, 变态人格者
vocabulary.context: In the novel, Edward Hyde represents the doctor's other self, a psychopath who does not conform to civilized society, and who shares a body with the doctor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 小说中，爱德华·海德代表了医生的另一个自我，一个不符合文明社会、与医生共享身体的精神病患者

vocabulary.definition: n. 承担者, 承办人, 企业家, 承办丧葬者, 殡仪员；[经] 承办商
vocabulary.context: Professional wrestlers, more often than not, perform under ring names depicting their alter egos, such as Terry Bollea (Hulk Hogan), Mark Calaway (The Undertaker), and Paul Levesque (Triple H).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 职业摔跤手通常会以代表他们另一个自我的擂台名进行表演，例如特里·博利亚（浩克·霍根）、马克·卡拉威（送葬者）和保罗·莱维斯克（Triple H）

vocabulary.definition: adv. 心理上, 从心理学角度
vocabulary.context: In literary analysis, the term alter ego sometimes refers to characters in different works who are psychologically similar, or a fictional character whose behavior, speech, or thoughts intentionally represent those of the author.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在文学分析中，"另一我"一词有时指不同作品中心理相似的人物，或者是行为、言语或思想有意代表作者的虚构人物

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使一致, 使遵守, 使符合；v.i. 一致, 符合, 适应
vocabulary.context: In the novel, Edward Hyde represents the doctor's other self, a psychopath who does not conform to civilized society, and who shares a body with the doctor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 小说中，爱德华·海德代表了医生的另一个自我，一个不符合文明社会、与医生共享身体的精神病患者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 同义词的, 同义的
vocabulary.context: Since this book was published, the names "Jekyll and Hyde" have become synonymous with a split personality, or an alter ego that becomes capable of overpowering the original self.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自从这本书出版以来，"杰基尔和海德"这个名字已经成为人格分裂的代名词，或者是能够压倒原始自我的另一个自我

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无情的, 残忍的, 毫不留情的, 彻底的；[法] 无情的, 残忍的
vocabulary.context: Sometimes, he is Harvey Dent, the clean-cut lawyer of Gotham City; other times, he is Two-Face, a ruthless monster who uses his dual-faced coin to determine who lives and dies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有时，他是哈维·登特，哥谭市干净利落的律师；有时，他是双面人，一个无情的怪物，用他的双面硬币来决定谁的生死

vocabulary.definition: adj. 传记的
vocabulary.context: A biographical TV-movie called Redemption: The Stan Tookie Williams Story was made in 2004, featuring Jamie Foxx as Williams.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一部名为《救赎：斯坦·图基·威廉姆斯的故事》的传记电视电影于 2004 年制作，由杰米·福克斯饰演威廉姆斯

vocabulary.definition: adj. 裁缝做的, 特制的
vocabulary.context: And I say to you and all those who can listen and will listen that redemption is tailor-made for the wretched, and that's what I used to be [...].
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我对你们以及所有能够倾听并愿意倾听的人说，救赎是为不幸的人量身定做的，而我曾经就是这样的[...]

vocabulary.definition: n. 层, 地层, 阶层；[医] 层
vocabulary.context: [...Redemption] is not predicated on color or race or social stratum or one's religious background.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: [...救赎]不以肤色、种族、社会阶层或宗教背景为基础

vocabulary.definition: n. 谓语, 谓项；adj. 谓语的；v.t. 断定为, 使基于, 意味着, 预言；v.i. 断言；[计] 谓词; 判定; 断定
vocabulary.context: [...Redemption] is not predicated on color or race or social stratum or one's religious background.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: [...救赎]不以肤色、种族、社会阶层或宗教背景为基础

vocabulary.context: The movement was unsuccessful, however; clemency was rejected by Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger, and on December 13, 2005, Stanley Williams was executed by lethal injection.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，这场运动没有成功；宽大处理被州长阿诺·施瓦辛格拒绝，2005年12月13日，斯坦利·威廉姆斯被注射死刑

vocabulary.definition: n. 草案, 礼仪, 协议；v. 拟定；[计] 协议, 协议列表实用程序
vocabulary.context: While in prison he also wrote a "peace protocol" to help rival gangs work out disagreements.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在监狱里，他还写了一份"和平协议"，帮助敌对帮派解决分歧

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 涉及, 含意, 暗示, 牵连；n. 包含的东西
vocabulary.context: He refused to help police investigate his gang, and he was implicated in multiple escape plots as well.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他拒绝帮助警方调查他的团伙，并且还参与了多起越狱阴谋

vocabulary.context: He became interested in weight lifting during this prison stint, and he became physically much bigger and more muscular.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在监狱服刑期间，他对举重产生了兴趣，他的体格变得更大，肌肉更发达

vocabulary.definition: n. 保护消费者利益运动
vocabulary.context: But even in countries where the desire for upward social mobility through consumerism is strong, the poor may not be able to better themselves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但即使在通过消费主义向上社会流动的愿望强烈的国家，穷人也可能无法改善自己

vocabulary.definition: n. 流行词
vocabulary.context: The term has been a commercial and cultural buzzword for over a century, but the phenomenon still resonates today.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个词一个多世纪以来一直是商业和文化流行语，但这种现象至今仍然引起共鸣

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使互相连接
vocabulary.context: Especially with society now more interconnected than ever, it is easy for people to notice - and become jealous of - what their neighbors have that they don't.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特别是现在社会的联系比以往任何时候都更加紧密，人们很容易注意到并嫉妒他们的邻居拥有而他们没有的东西

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使感觉不满, 不满足
vocabulary.context: Some people may not be able to "keep up with the Joneses" and feel dissatisfied or inferior.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有些人可能无法"攀比"，感到不满或自卑

vocabulary.definition: n. 有效性, 可利用性, 可利用的人；[计] 有效性; 可用性
vocabulary.context: In the United States, the widespread availability of luxury products such as cars, technology, and homes that show a person's status is one thing that has made social mobility possible.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在美国，显示一个人地位的汽车、技术和房屋等奢侈品的广泛使用是使社会流动性成为可能的原因之一

vocabulary.definition: n. 积聚, 累积, 积聚物；[医] 蓄积, 累积
vocabulary.context: On the other hand, it means that people in a society sometimes become preoccupied with the accumulation of wealth and status, and there may be winners and losers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一方面，这意味着社会中的人们有时会专注于财富和地位的积累，可能会有赢家和输家

vocabulary.definition: n. 突起, 突出, 显著, 突出物, 凸出；[医] 隆凸, 凸
vocabulary.context: And of those who did exit poverty, 15% of Americans were likely to make a round trip back under the poverty line, compared with 16% in Germany, 10% in the UK, and 7% in Canada." In other words, the more unequal the population of a country, the less likely people will be able to move up or down the ladder of social prominence and wealth.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在那些确实摆脱贫困的人中，有 15% 的美国人可能会再次回到贫困线以下，而德国的这一比例为 16%，英国为 10%，加拿大为 7%。"换句话说，一个国家的人口越不平等，人们在社会地位和财富的阶梯上上下移动的可能性就越小

vocabulary.definition: adj. 被先占的, 入神的, 一心一意的
vocabulary.context: On the other hand, it means that people in a society sometimes become preoccupied with the accumulation of wealth and status, and there may be winners and losers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一方面，这意味着社会中的人们有时会专注于财富和地位的积累，可能会有赢家和输家

vocabulary.definition: adj. 卓越的, 显著的, 突出的, 凸出的
vocabulary.context: One explanation is that the Joneses were a prominent, wealthy family from New York.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一种解释是琼斯家族是纽约的一个显赫而富有的家族

vocabulary.definition: n. [计] 副作用, 后效应( after-effect的复数形式 ); [化] 弹性后效; [医] 后效, 后作用
vocabulary.context: are the after-effects
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 是后遗症

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 梦见爱尔兰抽奖而死

vocabulary.contextTranslation: Aqui被称为negrito

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在线身份的可靠性和"面具"效应

vocabulary.context: Social networking services and online avatars have made the notion of identity far more complex, because the identities that people define in the social media are not necessarily the identities that they actually have.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 社交网络服务和在线头像使身份的概念变得更加复杂，因为人们在社交媒体中定义的身份不一定是他们实际拥有的身份

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与现实世界物理和感官约束的关系

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与现实世界物理和感官约束的关系

vocabulary.definition: adv. 含蓄, 不言明, 暗示, 暗指, 内含, 固有, 无疑, 绝对, 无保留；[计] 隐含地
vocabulary.context: Users express online identity both explicitly and implicitly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 用户以显式和隐式方式表达在线身份

vocabulary.definition: n. 理论家, 理论工作者
vocabulary.context: A person may feel that she is able to lie about her identity on the Internet because it creates a "mask" effect, where no one can see her "true self." Social theorists believe that whenever an individual interacts with others online, she portrays a mask of her identity, not her true identity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个人可能会觉得她能够在互联网上谎报自己的身份，因为这会产生一种"面具"效应，没有人能看到她的"真实自我"。社会理论家认为，每当一个人在网上与他人互动时，她都会描绘出自己身份的面具，而不是她的真实身份

vocabulary.definition: adj. 实际上起作用的, 虚的, 实质的, 有效的, 事实上生效的；[计] 虚拟
vocabulary.context: Most concerns about virtual identity revolve around the contrast between online and offline existence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对虚拟身份的大多数担忧都围绕着线上和线下存在之间的对比

vocabulary.definition: n. 交流, 交通, 通讯；[计] 通信
vocabulary.context: For example, several studies have shown that people lie about themselves in online dating profiles, or in communication with potential partners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，多项研究表明，人们会在在线约会资料中或与潜在伴侣的交流中撒谎

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当这种情况发生时，可能需要进行气管切开术来建立该气道

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当 TCOF1 基因发生突变时，会对核糖体 RNA (rRNA) 的产生产生负面影响

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非典型的；[医] 非典型的, 不标准的
vocabulary.context: People with Treacher Collins often have missing cheekbones, downward slanting eyes, hearing loss, atypical or missing ears, and a smaller jaw.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 患有特雷彻·柯林斯症的人通常颧骨缺失、眼睛向下倾斜、听力损失、耳朵不典型或缺失，以及下巴较小

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 患有特雷彻柯林斯综合征的人也经常在一岁左右接受腭裂手术

vocabulary.definition: adj. 天生的, 先天的；[医] 先天的
vocabulary.context: Treacher Collins syndrome, named for Edward Treacher Collins who first described its symptoms, is a rare congenital disorder that impacts facial features.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特雷彻柯林斯综合症，以爱德华特雷彻柯林斯的名字命名，他首先描述了其症状，是一种影响面部特征的罕见先天性疾病

vocabulary.definition: n. 船岸之间的水区, 锚地, 卧铺；v.i. 停泊；v.t. 使停泊
vocabulary.context: People tend to give wide berth to the things and people that they perceive as a threat to them - those people who are 'different' or who they don't understand.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人们倾向于对他们认为对他们构成威胁的事物和人--那些"不同"或他们不理解的人--保持距离

vocabulary.definition: n. 并发症状, 综合征, 同时存在的事物；[计] 校验子; 并发位
vocabulary.context: Treacher Collins syndrome, named for Edward Treacher Collins who first described its symptoms, is a rare congenital disorder that impacts facial features.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特雷彻柯林斯综合症，以爱德华特雷彻柯林斯的名字命名，他首先描述了其症状，是一种影响面部特征的罕见先天性疾病

vocabulary.definition: n. 外部, 外面；adj. 外部的, 客观的, 表面的
vocabulary.context: Shortly after, patients often require ear reconstruction or an external device to make it easier to hear clearly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 不久之后，患者经常需要进行耳朵重建或外部设备，以使其更容易清晰地听到声音

vocabulary.definition: n. 报偿, 酬金, 赔偿；v.t. 报偿, 赔偿, 报酬
vocabulary.context: She would recompense investors by giving them a percentage of the money she made from the store's business.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她会从商店业务中赚取一定比例的收入来回报投资者

vocabulary.definition: n. 宣言, 声明
vocabulary.context: One writer named Karl Marx wrote a book called The Communist Manifesto , which told workers to rebel against business owners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一位名叫卡尔·马克思的作家写了一本名为《共产党宣言》的书，其中告诉工人要反抗企业主

vocabulary.definition: n. 建议者, 支持者；[法] 提议者, 辩护者, 支持者
vocabulary.context: Capitalist proponents believe capitalism is the best system for the economy because it can help the most people.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 资本主义支持者认为资本主义是最好的经济体系，因为它可以帮助大多数人

vocabulary.definition: n. 投资者；[经] 投资者

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她会从商店业务中赚取一定比例的收入来回报投资者

vocabulary.definition: n. 资本主义；[经] 资本主义
vocabulary.context: Capitalism is an economic system in which individual people make most of the decisions and own most of the property in a country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 资本主义是一种由个人做出大部分决定并拥有国家大部分财产的经济体系

vocabulary.definition: n. 资本家, 资本主义者；adj. 资本主义的
vocabulary.context: Capitalists say the government should not make very many laws about the economy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 资本家说政府不应该制定太多有关经济的法律

vocabulary.definition: n. 投资；[医] 包埋料, 围模料, 包埋法, 围模法
vocabulary.context: Another important part of capitalism is investment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 资本主义的另一个重要组成部分是投资

vocabulary.definition: n. 规则, 管理, 调整；[计] 调整; 规章; 规则; 调节
vocabulary.context: They prefer "free markets," which are markets with only a few laws and regulations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们更喜欢"自由市场"，即只有很少法律和法规的市场

vocabulary.definition: n. 面孔的一部分(如眼、口等), 特征, 容貌, 特色, 特写；v.t. 是...的特色, 特写, 放映；v.i. 起重要作用；[计] 特性
vocabulary.context: The features of capitalism may sound familiar to you, because capitalism is the type of economic system that exists in many countries today, including the United States, Canada, and Great Britain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 资本主义的特征你可能听起来很熟悉，因为资本主义是当今许多国家都存在的经济体系类型，包括美国、加拿大和英国

vocabulary.definition: n. 有选举权的人, 选举人；[法] 有选举权者, 合格选举人
vocabulary.context: Each state has a group of people called electors who represent the voters in their state.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每个州都有一群称为选举人的人，他们代表本州的选民

vocabulary.definition: n. 分解, 溶解, 解散, 结束；[化] 溶解; 溶解作用
vocabulary.context: A war and an economic crisis had led to the dissolution of old political parties, so four candidates thought they had a good shot at winning.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 战争和经济危机导致旧政党解散，因此四位候选人认为他们很有机会获胜

vocabulary.definition: adv. 果断地；决然地
vocabulary.context: Each candidate would need to win in Florida to decisively win the election.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每位候选人都需要在佛罗里达州获胜才能决定性地赢得选举

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不可预知的
vocabulary.context: The election of 1948 reminded everyone just how unpredictable presidential politics can be.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1948 年的选举提醒所有人，总统政治是多么难以预测

vocabulary.definition: n. 评论员, 实况广播员, 注释者, 时事评论员
vocabulary.context: The election of 1948 reminded everyone just how unpredictable presidential politics can be. Every political commentator expected Thomas E. Dewey, a Republican, to win the race easily. People were so sure about it that newspapers printed headlines and articles about it before the polls even closed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1948 年的选举提醒所有人，总统政治是多么难以预测。每个政治评论家都期望共和党人托马斯·E·杜威能够轻松赢得竞选。人们对此非常确定，以至于在投票结束之前报纸就刊登了有关它的头条新闻和文章

vocabulary.definition: adj. 命令的, 托管的；[经] 受托者, 命令者
vocabulary.context: They declared that a mandatory recount was unconstitutional, and therefore, the original results must stand.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们宣称强制重新计票是违宪的，因此最初的结果必须成立

vocabulary.definition: adv. 作为整个民族, 举国一致地, 全国性地
vocabulary.context: This angered many Democrats, since Al Gore received 540,000 more popular votes nationally than Bush; however, it is the Electoral College that really decides who will be president.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这激怒了许多民主党人，因为阿尔·戈尔在全国范围内获得的选票比布什多 54 万张；然而，真正决定谁将成为总统的是选举团

vocabulary.definition: n. 不信任, 疑惑；v. 不信任, 疑惑
vocabulary.context: Tensions were still high between the North and the South, and Republicans and Democrats still mistrusted each other.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 南北之间的紧张局势依然严重，共和党和民主党仍然相互不信任

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 为政党利益改划选区；v.i. 不公正地划分选区；n. 改变选举区
vocabulary.context: It also prohibits gerrymandering, the practice of redrawing voting districts to have as few minority voters as possible.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它还禁止选区划分选区，即重新划定选区以尽可能减少少数族裔选民的做法

Living up to its namesake, The Windy City, the powder covered the Wrigley Building and more than 100 cars instead.

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 种族平等大会（CORE）是由詹姆斯·法默（James Farmer）领导的北方学生团体，该团体也支持直接行动

vocabulary.definition: n. 正义, 公正, 正直
vocabulary.context: Sit-in organizers believed that if the violence were only on the part of the white community, the world would see the righteousness of their cause.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 静坐组织者相信，如果暴力只发生在白人社区，世界就会看到他们事业的正义性

vocabulary.definition: v. bl.retake的过去式
vocabulary.context: Without their support, Reconstruction state governments fell, Democrats retook positions in local governments, and southern whites renewed their efforts to strip African-Americans of their rights.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有他们的支持，重建州政府垮台，民主党重新占据地方政府职位，南方白人重新努力剥夺非裔美国人的权利

vocabulary.definition: adj. 战前的；adv. 在战前
vocabulary.context: Recently freed African-Americans found the postwar South very similar to the prewar South.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最近获释的非裔美国人发现战后的南方与战前的南方非常相似

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 控告, 怀疑, 检举, 弹劾；[法] 控告, 检举, 弹劾
vocabulary.context: It was the Radical Republicans who impeached President Johnson in 1868.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1868年弹劾约翰逊总统的是激进共和党人

vocabulary.definition: num. 第十五, 十五分之一
vocabulary.context: And finally, the Fifteenth Amendment gave African-American men the right to vote.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最后，第十五修正案赋予非裔美国男性投票权

vocabulary.definition: adj. 战后的
vocabulary.context: Recently freed African-Americans found the postwar South very similar to the prewar South.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最近获释的非裔美国人发现战后的南方与战前的南方非常相似

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非正式的, 不拘礼的, 通俗的；[经] 非正式的, 日常使用的
vocabulary.context: Finally, through informal meetings, Democrats and Republicans agreed to the Compromise of 1877.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最后，通过非正式会议，民主党和共和党同意了1877年妥协案

vocabulary.definition: n. 作梦（歌名）
vocabulary.context: Grant was a revered and decorated Union general during the Civil War, yet turned out to be less effective as a politician.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 格兰特在内战期间是一位受人尊敬且功勋卓著的联邦将军，但事实证明他作为政治家的效率较低

vocabulary.definition: n. 丑闻, 中伤, 耻辱, 反感, 流言蜚语；[法] 丑事, 丑闻, 干丑事的人
vocabulary.context: From 1869 to 1876, the Republican President Grant faced multiple scandals and corruption that allowed Democrats to gain seats and power in Congress.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从1869年到1876年，共和党总统格兰特面临多重丑闻和腐败，这使得民主党在国会获得了席位和权力

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使成联邦, 使同盟
vocabulary.context: He federalized the Arkansas National Guard in order to remove the soldiers from Faubus's control.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他将阿肯色州国民警卫队联邦化，以使士兵脱离福布斯的控制

vocabulary.definition: n. 难局, 僵局, 死路, 死胡同
vocabulary.context: Board of Education Supreme Court decision could lead to an impasse between the federal government and the states.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最高法院教育委员会的裁决可能会导致联邦政府和各州之间陷入僵局

vocabulary.definition: adj. 发怒的, 生气的
vocabulary.context: Word of this spread throughout the community, and a thousand irate citizens stormed the school grounds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这件事传遍了整个社区，一千名愤怒的公民冲进了学校场地

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无情地, 残忍地
vocabulary.context: An astonished America watched footage of brutish, white southerners mercilessly harassing African American children calmly walking into school, intent on getting an education.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 震惊的美国人看到了野蛮的南方白人无情地骚扰平静地走进学校、一心想接受教育的非裔美国儿童的镜头

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 倾听
vocabulary.context: The situation hearkened back to the dangerous federal-state conflicts of the 19th century that followed the end of the Civil War.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种情况让人回想起19世纪内战结束后危险的联邦与州冲突

vocabulary.definition: adj. 畜生般的, 愚钝的, 粗野的, 残忍的, 野蛮的
vocabulary.context: An astonished America watched footage of brutish, white southerners mercilessly harassing African American children calmly walking into school, intent on getting an education.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 震惊的美国人看到了野蛮的南方白人无情地骚扰平静地走进学校、一心想接受教育的非裔美国儿童的镜头

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 动员, 调动, 使流通；v.i. 动员
vocabulary.context: Under the pretext of maintaining order but in support of the segregationists, Arkansas Governor Orval Faubus mobilized the Arkansas National Guard to prevent the Little Rock Nine from entering the school.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿肯色州州长奥瓦尔·福布斯以维持秩序为借口，支持种族隔离主义者，动员阿肯色州国民警卫队阻止小石城九人进入学校

vocabulary.definition: n. 监督, 管理；[经] 监督, 管理
vocabulary.context: For the next few months, the African American students attended school under armed supervision.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 接下来的几个月里，非裔美国学生在武装监督下上学

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未经决定的, 未确定的, 性质未明的, 形式未定的, 缺乏决断力的；[计] 未定的
vocabulary.context: But many factors undetermined at the outbreak of war could have tilted the balance sheet toward a different outcome.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但战争爆发时许多不确定的因素可能会使资产负债表向不同的结果倾斜

vocabulary.definition: n. 毅力, 意志力
vocabulary.context: Still, the Confederacy was not without resources and willpower.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管如此，南部邦联并非没有资源和意志力

vocabulary.definition: adj. 资源丰富的, 策略的, 机智的；[法] 资源丰富的, 善于遂机应变的, 足智多谋的
vocabulary.context: The South also proved to be very resourceful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实证明，南方也非常足智多谋

vocabulary.definition: n. 统计量；adj. 统计的, 统计学的
vocabulary.context: But that statistic was misleading.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但该统计数据具有误导性

vocabulary.definition: n. 火药；[化] 有烟火药; 火药
vocabulary.context: All of the principal ingredients of gunpowder were imported.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 火药的所有主要成分都是进口的

vocabulary.definition: n. 核心, 核子, 原子核, 起点；[计] 核心程序
vocabulary.context: The South also had a great nucleus of trained officers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 南方也有一支训练有素的军官队伍

vocabulary.definition: n. 阻塞；v.t. 封锁
vocabulary.context: A blockade could suffocate the South.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 封锁可能会让南方窒息

vocabulary.definition: adj. 引入歧途的, 使人误解的, 骗人的；[法] 误写姓名的, 误称的, 令人误解的
vocabulary.context: But that statistic was misleading.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但该统计数据具有误导性

vocabulary.definition: adj. 巨大的, 优秀的, 可怕的, 艰难的
vocabulary.context: It had to destroy the South's capacity and will to resist - a formidable challenge in any war.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它必须摧毁南方的抵抗能力和意志--这在任何战争中都是一个巨大的挑战

vocabulary.definition: n. 重商主义, 商业理论, 商业活动, 商界习俗, 商用术语；[经] 重商主义, 商业活动, 商用术语
vocabulary.context: Farmers began producing surplus crops that allowed others to move their concentration from farming to pursuing other trades, such as mercantilism or skilled craftwork.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 农民开始生产剩余作物，使其他人能够将注意力从农业转移到其他行业，例如重商主义或熟练手工艺

vocabulary.definition: n. 工艺(品)
vocabulary.context: Farmers began producing surplus crops that allowed others to move their concentration from farming to pursuing other trades, such as mercantilism or skilled craftwork.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 农民开始生产剩余作物，使其他人能够将注意力从农业转移到其他行业，例如重商主义或熟练手工艺

vocabulary.definition: n. 试金石, 检验标准
vocabulary.context: Eventually, pyramids, mummies, and great cities became touchstones of this flourishing culture.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最终，金字塔、木乃伊和大城市成为了这种繁荣文化的试金石

vocabulary.definition: adj. 黏合在一起的；[医] 内聚的
vocabulary.context: that the valley's inhabitants began to form a cohesive civilization.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 山谷里的居民开始形成有凝聚力的文明

vocabulary.definition: n. 刈幅, 刈下的一行草, 刈下的一行麦, 行, 一条, 条, 一行
vocabulary.context: But along the Nile River was a fertile swath that proved - and still proves - a life source for many Egyptians.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但尼罗河沿岸有一片肥沃的土地，过去和现在都证明是许多埃及人的生命源泉

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 制造, 建造, 装配, 伪造
vocabulary.context: Egyptian artisans created copper tools such as chisels and needles - all new inventions - that allowed them to fabricate ornamental jewelry.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 埃及工匠创造了凿子和针等铜工具--所有新发明--使他们能够制造装饰性珠宝

vocabulary.definition: n. 剩余, 过剩, 盈余；adj. 过剩的, 剩余的
vocabulary.context: Farmers began producing surplus crops that allowed others to move their concentration from farming to pursuing other trades, such as mercantilism or skilled craftwork.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 农民开始生产剩余作物，使其他人能够将注意力从农业转移到其他行业，例如重商主义或熟练手工艺

vocabulary.definition: adj. 适当的, 相配的；[法] 合适的, 适宜的, 适当的
vocabulary.context: As the river receded, it deposited a rich, brown layer of silt that was suitable for growing wheat, beans, barley, or even cotton.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着河流退去，沉积了一层丰富的棕色淤泥，适合种植小麦、豆类、大麦，甚至棉花

vocabulary.definition: n. 吉兆, 占卜, 赞助
vocabulary.context: Refugees fall under the auspice of the U.N.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 难民受到联合国的支持

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无伴侣的, 无伴随的, 无伴奏的
vocabulary.context: In 2016 alone, according to UNHCR, 75,000 children applied for asylum as "unaccompanied minors." Most were from Afghanistan and Syria.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据联合国难民署称，仅2016年就有75,000名儿童作为"无人陪伴的未成年人"申请庇护。大多数来自阿富汗和叙利亚

vocabulary.definition: adj. 中等收入的
vocabulary.context: The greatest burden of the global refugee crisis falls on low-income and middle-income countries.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 全球难民危机的最大负担落在低收入和中等收入国家身上

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 连根拔起, 根除
vocabulary.context: This includes 10.3 million people who were uprooted from their homes in 2016.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这包括2016年背井离乡的1,030万人

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 遮阴, 使阴暗, 使失色, 使相形见绌, 使蒙上阴影
vocabulary.context: In 2016 there were 40.3 million internally displaced persons around the world, far overshadowing the 25.3 million refugees.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2016年，全球有4030万境内流离失所者，远远超过2530万难民

vocabulary.definition: adv. 强制地, 用力地
vocabulary.context: The number of people forcibly displaced from their homes is the highest since World War II.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 被迫流离失所的人数达到了二战以来的最高水平

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 指定, 指明, 称呼；adj. 已选出而未上任的
vocabulary.context: designates them as "internally displaced persons" or IDPs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 将他们称为"国内流离失所者"或IDP

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 逃避, 逃跑, 逃走；v.i. 逃, 消失
vocabulary.context: Many of these kids are fleeing alone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中许多孩子正在独自逃离

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 给...权利, 取名为, 给予名称, 叫做；[法] 给...权利, 使有资格, 称呼
vocabulary.context: High Commissioner for Refugees and are entitled to certain rights and basic assistance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 难民事务高级专员有权享有某些权利和基本援助

vocabulary.definition: adj. 一次性的；n. 一次性事物
vocabulary.context: Clearly, one-off events or inconsequential mistakes - taking the wrong turn in a foreign city or being late to a party with friends - don't make the best candidates (hence the saying "don't sweat the small stuff").
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 显然，一次性事件或无关紧要的错误--在外国城市走错路或与朋友聚会迟到--并不是最好的候选人（因此有句俗语"别为小事操心"）

vocabulary.definition: n. 混合者, 混合器, 搅拌器；[化] 掺合机; 捏和机; 混合机; 搅拌机; 搅切器; 捣碎器
vocabulary.context: In one, we asked our participants to search the internet for the lowest price for a particular blender brand and model (with the possibility of winning a cash price if they were successful).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中，我们要求参与者在互联网上搜索特定搅拌机品牌和型号的最低价格（如果成功，有可能赢得现金价格）

vocabulary.definition: n. 自我改善, 自我修养
vocabulary.context: Could deciding to focus on the negative emotions associated with failure lead to thoughts about self-improvement - and, with time, actual improvement?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 决定关注与失败相关的负面情绪是否会导致自我完善的想法 - 并且随着时间的推移，实际的改进？

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使合理, 合理地处理, 使有理化；v.i. 合理地思考
vocabulary.context: There's a reason people tend to instinctively rationalize or have self-protective thoughts after they've made a mistake.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人们在犯错后往往会本能地合理化或产生自我保护的想法，这是有原因的

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 赎回, 挽回, 恢复, 偿还, 补偿；[经] 履行(契约), 买回, 赎回
vocabulary.context: In the studies, we used something called a two-stage paradigm: First participants attempted a task in which they failed; then - after series of unrelated tasks - they would have the opportunity to redeem themselves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在研究中，我们使用了一种称为两阶段范式的方法：首先，参与者尝试了一项失败的任务；然后，参与者尝试了一项失败的任务。然后--在一系列不相关的任务之后--他们将有机会救赎自己

vocabulary.definition: n. 装备, 帆装；v.t. 装配, 装扮, 给船装帆, 垄断, 操纵
vocabulary.context: In reality, the task was rigged.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，这项任务是被操纵的

vocabulary.definition: v. 使（人或人的身体）非常虚弱( debilitate的现在分词 )
vocabulary.context: It would be debilitating if you were to focus on how bad you felt after each failure, big and small.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果你在每次失败（无论大小）之后只关注自己的感受有多糟糕，那将会使你变得虚弱

vocabulary.definition: n. 主流
vocabulary.context: Academics and the mainstream media tend to offer a simple solution: Don't let it get to you and think about how things could have been worse.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 学术界和主流媒体倾向于提供一个简单的解决方案：不要让它影响你，并思考事情可能会变得更糟

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使分裂成若干对立的小国；使割据；v.i. 分割成小国；割据
vocabulary.context: In the 1800s, unions covered only a small fraction of workers and were balkanized and relatively weak.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在 1800 年代，工会只覆盖一小部分工人，而且是割裂的、相对薄弱的

vocabulary.definition: n. 一星期工作时间
vocabulary.context: They also focused on getting workers more days off, such as the Labor Day holiday, and reducing the workweek to just six days.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们还致力于让工人有更多的休息日，例如劳动节假期，并将每周工作时间减少到六天

vocabulary.definition: n. （法）精神, 才智；机敏
vocabulary.context: McGuire, who founded the New York City Brotherhood of Carpenters and Joiners, in 1881 with suggesting a date that would fall "nearly midway between the Fourth of July and Thanksgiving" that "would publicly show the strength and esprit de corps of the trade and labor organizations."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 麦奎尔于 1881 年创立了纽约市木匠和细木工兄弟会，并建议将一个日期定为"几乎在美国独立日和感恩节中间"，以"公开展示贸易和劳工组织的力量和团队精神"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 白领阶层的
vocabulary.context: Although the battle over these issues would seem to have been won long ago, this issue is starting to come back with a vengeance, not for manufacturing workers but for highly skilled white-collar workers, many of whom are constantly connected to work.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管这些问题的斗争似乎早就胜利了，但这个问题又开始卷土重来，不是针对制造业工人，而是针对高技能白领工人，他们中的许多人一直与工作联系在一起

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 弄短, 缩短, 减少；v.i. 缩小, 变短
vocabulary.context: Shortening the work week was one way of turning the working class into the consuming class.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 缩短工作周是将工人阶级转变为消费阶级的一种方式

vocabulary.definition: n. 减少, 缩影, 变化；[化] 还原
vocabulary.context: The founders were looking for two things: a means of unifying union workers and a reduction in work time.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 创始人们正在寻找两件事：团结工会工人的手段和减少工作时间

vocabulary.definition: n. 制造业；adj. 制造业的
vocabulary.context: In the 1830s, manufacturing workers were putting in 70-hour weeks on average.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在 1830 年代，制造业工人平均每周工作 70 小时

vocabulary.definition: adj. 适度的, 稳健的, 中等的, 节制的；v.t. 节制, 减轻, 使缓和；v.i. 变缓和, 主持会议
vocabulary.context: More moderate trade union members, however, advocated for a September Labor Day of parades and picnics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，较为温和的工会成员主张在九月劳动节举行游行和野餐

vocabulary.definition: adv. 非公认地；非正式地
vocabulary.context: "Officially or unofficially?"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "官方还是非正式？"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 想入非非的, 不切实的；n. 疯子, 怪人, 狂想家
vocabulary.context: "They'll be considered crackpots if they do.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "如果他们这样做，就会被认为是疯子

vocabulary.definition: adv. 非言语所能表达地
vocabulary.context: Imagine how unbearably, how unutterably cold the Universe would be if one were all alone..."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 想象一下，如果一个人独自一人，宇宙将是多么难以忍受、多么难以形容的寒冷......"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有感觉力的, 意识到的；n. 有知觉的人
vocabulary.context: You're asking me to believe in sentient meat."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你是在要求我相信有感知的肉。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 极小的, 极微的, 无限小的；n. 极小量, 极微量, 无限小
vocabulary.context: Infinitesimal, in fact."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，无穷小。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 四分之一圆, 象限, 象限仪；[医] 四分体, 象限
vocabulary.context: "Officially, we are required to contact, welcome and log in any and all sentient races or multibeings in this quadrant of the Universe, without prejudice, fear or favor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "正式地，我们被要求接触、欢迎和登录宇宙这一象限中的任何和所有有知觉的种族或多元生命，没有偏见、恐惧或偏爱

vocabulary.definition: n. 预期生命期限；寿命；预期使用期限
vocabulary.context: Do you have any idea what's the lifespan of meat?"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你知道肉的寿命有多长吗？"

vocabulary.definition: n. 扇形, 部门, 部分, 函数尺, 象限仪, 段, 区段；v.t. 把...分成扇形；[计] 扇面; 扇区; 段; 区段
vocabulary.context: These creatures are the only sentient race in that sector and they're made out of meat."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些生物是该区域唯一有知觉的种族，它们是由肉制成的。"

vocabulary.definition: adv. 宁可, 稍微, 相当
vocabulary.context: "Yes, a rather shy but sweet hydrogen core cluster intelligence in a class nine star in G445 zone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "是的，G445区的九级恒星中有一个相当害羞但甜蜜的氢核心星团智慧

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家还发现，气味对于婴儿和新父母来说都起着非常重要的作用，并且可能帮助我们在生命之初形成更牢固的家庭依恋！

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些感觉首先到达丘脑，丘脑是大脑的邮局，信息在这里被分类，然后发送到大脑的相关部分，包括处理情绪的杏仁核和存储记忆的海马体

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它与我们同在，深深地潜伏在我们的大脑中，它给我们带来甜蜜和苦涩的记忆，让我们惊讶不已

vocabulary.contextTranslation: "但是，由于我们燃烧了大量的化石燃料并砍伐了地球上的森林，我们增加了温室气体的数量，结果改变了地球的温度。"摘自《学生科学新闻》，2010 年

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，通过砍伐树木和森林用于农田和其他人类用途，更多的二氧化碳也被添加到大气中

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 二氧化碳的增加"基本上完全是由于燃料的燃烧"，苏珊·所罗门说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不肥沃的, 不毛的, 不结果实的
vocabulary.context: He even suggests a humane way to kill off a rat population that threatened local bird species: Add a gene that makes the rats infertile.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他甚至提出了一种人道的方法来消灭威胁当地鸟类的老鼠种群：添加一种使老鼠不育的基因

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 实施现场试验
vocabulary.context: Also, he asks, "where do you field-test a gene drive?" After all, the animals people want to change don't stay put.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，他问道："你们在哪里现场测试基因驱动器？"毕竟，人们想要改变的动物不会原地踏步

vocabulary.context: For instance, Esvelt notes, "Rats are really good stowaways [on ships]." Or people could intentionally move them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，艾斯维尔特指出，"老鼠确实是[船上]的好偷渡者。"或者人们可以故意移动它们

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未被改变的, 不变的, 照旧的, 依然如此的
vocabulary.context: When the egg or sperm from the organism with the gene drive fuses to an egg or sperm from an unaltered organism, the gene drive goes to work.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当来自带有基因驱动的生物体的卵子或精子与来自未改变的生物体的卵子或精子融合时，基因驱动开始发挥作用

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 科学家们已经使用这种 RNA-酶组合来破坏和修复多种动物的基因

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 19世纪，欧洲商人和移民将许多外国老鼠、白鼬和其他动物引入了这个南太平洋岛国

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有时，修复会使用基因的健康副本作为模式来修复先前损坏的基因

vocabulary.definition: adj. 在飞行中的
vocabulary.context: The small drops might have broken off larger drops in-flight.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 小水滴可能会在飞行中分解成较大的水滴

vocabulary.definition: n. 做空中造型动作的跳伞运动员
vocabulary.context: (Velocity is a measurement of how fast and in which direction an object moves.) Every object falling through the atmosphere, from skydivers to hailstones, has a terminal velocity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: （速度是对物体移动速度和方向的测量。）从跳伞者到冰雹，从大气层中落下的每个物体都有一个终端速度

vocabulary.definition: n. 冰雹块
vocabulary.context: (Velocity is a measurement of how fast and in which direction an object moves.) Every object falling through the atmosphere, from skydivers to hailstones, has a terminal velocity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: （速度是对物体移动速度和方向的测量。）从跳伞者到冰雹，从大气层中落下的每个物体都有一个终端速度

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，科学家观察到雨滴的下落速度快于其最终速度

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 直径大于 0.5 毫米（0.02 英寸）的雨滴以每秒几米（英尺）的终端速度落下

vocabulary.definition: n. 细雨；v. 下毛毛雨

vocabulary.context: Shortly after his death, his plays were published in a collection known as the First Folio (1623), with a poem by Ben Jonson included that featured the lines, "He was not of an age, but for all time!" The memory of Shakespeare remained strong among audiences as well, since his plays were produced regularly by many companies.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他去世后不久，他的戏剧出版在名为《第一对开本》（First Folio，1623）的文集中，其中收录了本·琼森（Ben Jonson）的一首诗，其中的诗句是："他不属于一个时代，但属于所有时代！"观众对莎士比亚的记忆也很深刻，因为许多公司定期制作他的戏剧

vocabulary.definition: n. Frances 的爱称；[口]屁股；女子救护队队员
vocabulary.context: Multivolume editions of his plays were published, exuberant productions and extravagant sets supported stars such as Fanny Kemble and Edmund Kean, and touring companies brought small-scale versions of Shakespeare to towns and villages everywhere.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的戏剧出版了多卷本，丰富的制作和奢华的布景为范妮·肯布尔和埃德蒙·基恩等明星提供了支持，巡回演出公司将小规模版本的莎士比亚带到了各地的城镇和村庄

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的戏剧出版了多卷本，丰富的制作和奢华的布景为范妮·肯布尔和埃德蒙·基恩等明星提供了支持，巡回演出公司将小规模版本的莎士比亚带到了各地的城镇和村庄

vocabulary.definition: n. 哈比(希腊神话中残酷贪婪者)
vocabulary.context: Although usually beautiful, Ariel also appears before humans as a harpy, a frightening classical monster, half-human, half-bird, that tormented starving mortals by fouling their food.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然爱丽儿通常很美丽，但她也以鹰身女妖的身份出现在人类面前，这是一种可怕的古典怪物，半人半鸟，通过弄脏食物来折磨饥饿的凡人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 来世的, 阴间的, 空想的, 超自然的
vocabulary.context: Shakespeare drew inspiration for these otherworldly characters from oral tales filled with fairies, imps, hobgoblins, boggarts, elves, and many other spirits often believed not good enough to be angels or bad enough to be devils.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莎士比亚从口头故事中汲取了这些超凡脱俗人物的灵感，这些故事充满了仙女、小鬼、大妖精、博格特、精灵和许多其他通常被认为不够好成为天使或坏到无法成为魔鬼的灵魂

vocabulary.definition: n. 漂亮, 漂亮的人
vocabulary.context: Less interested in empty prettiness, modern audiences appreciate otherworldly mischief and mystery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现代观众对空洞的美丽不太感兴趣，而是欣赏超凡脱俗的恶作剧和神秘

vocabulary.definition: n. 妖怪, 幽灵
vocabulary.context: Robin, also called Puck, is a traditional, mischievous hobgoblin who changes shape, enjoys misleading travelers, and plays pranks on unsuspecting mortals.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 罗宾，也称为帕克，是一个传统的、顽皮的妖精，他会改变形状，喜欢误导旅行者，并对毫无戒心的凡人进行恶作剧

vocabulary.definition: adv. 顺从地；服从地；忠顺地
vocabulary.context: He serves a mortal master, Prospero, obediently but reluctantly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他服从凡人主人普洛斯彼罗，但又不情愿

vocabulary.definition: n. 仲夏, 夏至期；adj. 仲夏的
vocabulary.context: In Shakespeare's magical plays A Midsummer Night's Dream and The Tempest, fairies and sprites work magic and dabble for a time in human affairs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在莎士比亚的魔幻剧《仲夏夜之梦》和《暴风雨》中，仙女和精灵施展魔法，并一度涉足人类事务

vocabulary.definition: n. 暴风雨, 骚动；v.t. 使受大风暴影响, 使骚动, 使激动
vocabulary.context: In Shakespeare's magical plays A Midsummer Night's Dream and The Tempest, fairies and sprites work magic and dabble for a time in human affairs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在莎士比亚的魔幻剧《仲夏夜之梦》和《暴风雨》中，仙女和精灵施展魔法，并一度涉足人类事务

vocabulary.definition: n. 复活, 复兴, 文艺复兴；adj. 文艺复兴的
vocabulary.context: Oberon and Robin Goodfellow, two of Shakespeare's main fairies in A Midsummer "Oberon, Titania and Puck with Fairies Night's Dream, are traditional Renaissance Dancing" by William Blake is in the public characters.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莎士比亚笔下的两位主要仙女奥伯伦和罗宾·古德费罗在威廉·布莱克的《仲夏夜之梦》中的《奥伯伦、泰坦妮娅和帕克与仙女之夜的梦，是文艺复兴时期的传统舞蹈》中的公开角色

vocabulary.definition: n. 个性, 字符, 人物, 性质, 品格, 资格；[计] 字符
vocabulary.context: Shakespeare drew inspiration for these otherworldly characters from oral tales filled with fairies, imps, hobgoblins, boggarts, elves, and many other spirits often believed not good enough to be angels or bad enough to be devils.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莎士比亚从口头故事中汲取了这些超凡脱俗人物的灵感，这些故事充满了仙女、小鬼、大妖精、博格特、精灵和许多其他通常被认为不够好成为天使或坏到无法成为魔鬼的灵魂

vocabulary.definition: n. 遗传学家；[医] 遗传学家
vocabulary.context: He's a medical geneticist in England at Imperial College London.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他是英国伦敦帝国理工学院的医学遗传学家。 【08】他是一个没有头发的僵尸！"

vocabulary.definition: n. 蜜蜂
vocabulary.context: What if a "James Bond-style villain" used a similar gene drive to attack honeybees or other beneficial insects, says Philipp Messer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Philipp Messer 说，如果"詹姆斯·邦德式的恶棍"使用类似的基因驱动来攻击蜜蜂或其他有益昆虫会怎样

vocabulary.definition: n. 合著者, 共同执笔者
vocabulary.context: However, "no resistance was observed," says study coauthor Andrea Crisanti.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，"没有观察到耐药性"，研究合著者安德里亚·克里桑蒂说

vocabulary.definition: n. 变化, 突变, 元音变化；[化] 突变
vocabulary.context: But in those experiments, DNA changes, or mutations, that destroy the cutting site for CRISPR/Cas9 popped up.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但在这些实验中，出现了破坏 CRISPR/Cas9 切割位点的 DNA 变化或突变

vocabulary.definition: adj. 小比例尺的, 小规模的；[法] 小规模的
vocabulary.context: These new results come from a small-scale laboratory study.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些新结果来自一项小规模的实验室研究

vocabulary.definition: adj. 分子的, 由分子组成的；[医] 分子的
vocabulary.context: Gene drives use the molecular "scissors" known as CRISPR/Cas9 to copy and paste themselves into an organism's DNA at precise locations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 基因驱动使用称为 CRISPR/Cas9 的分子"剪刀"将自身复制并粘贴到生物体 DNA 的精确位置

vocabulary.definition: n. 疟疾, 瘴气；[医] 疟[疾]

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 基因编辑可能会导致一种携带疟疾的蚊子灭绝

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不毛的, 不孕的, 无菌的, 贫瘠的, 无效的, 枯燥的；[医] 无菌的, 消毒的; 不育的
vocabulary.context: Females with these broken genes were sterile and couldn't pass the mutations on to the next generation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 带有这些损坏基因的雌性是不育的，无法将突变传递给下一代

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 根除, 扑灭, 根绝, 消灭
vocabulary.context: "If you have a technology that could eradicate that [mosquito], it would be unethical not to use it," says Omar Akbari.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "如果你有一种技术可以消灭这种[蚊子]，那么不使用它就是不道德的，"奥马尔·阿克巴里说

vocabulary.definition: v. 劝诫, 忠告
vocabulary.context: Let her only exhort and persuade him,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就让她劝劝他，劝说他，

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但要温顺地屈服于他，

vocabulary.definition: n. 有礼, 优雅

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 彼此以礼相待；

vocabulary.definition: n. 惯用右手的人, 右手打击
vocabulary.context: ...Rookie of the Year; Orlando Cepeda, a Puerto Rican slugger, the 1958 National League Rookie of the Year; Juan Marichal, a Dominican right-hander and top pitcher of the 1960s; Tony Oliva, a Cuban slugger who racked up multiple batting titles, and was one of most feared hitters of the late 1960s, and Rod Carew, a Panamanian batting champion on the...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...年度最佳新秀；奥兰多·塞佩达（Orlando Cepeda），波多黎各重击手，1958年国家联盟年度最佳新秀；胡安·马里查尔 (Juan Marichal)，多米尼加右投手，20 世纪 60 年代的顶级投手；托尼·奥利瓦 (Tony Oliva) 是古巴击球手，多次获得击球冠军，是 20 世纪 60 年代末最令人畏惧的击球手之一，罗德·卡鲁 (Rod Carew) 是巴拿马击球冠军

vocabulary.definition: adj. 造成偏见的, 有侵害的；[法] 有损害的, 不利的, 有偏见的
vocabulary.context: A leader both on and off the field, the highly admired player protested prejudicial treatment of Latin American players and also led humanitarian missions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这位备受尊敬的球员是球场内外的领袖，他抗议拉丁美洲球员受到的偏见待遇，并领导了人道主义任务

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据《体育画报》和美国棒球研究协会 (SABR) 报道，这位多才多艺的内野手以其勇气和敏捷性而闻名，他毫不费力地抓住了最严重的直线突破和滚滚球，而那是在球员戴棒球手套之前

vocabulary.definition: adj. 大的, 非常的, 主要的, 重大的, 崇高的, 伟大的；adv. 顺利地, 得意地；n. 全部, 大人物, 大师

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 拉丁美洲产生了许多棒球巨星 - 这对美国职业棒球大联盟来说是一个巨大的胜利

vocabulary.definition: adj. 多才多艺的, 万用的, 万向的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 酸的, 酸臭的, 发酵的, 愠怒的, 讨厌的, 拙劣的, 不健全的；v.i. 变酸, 发酵, 厌烦, 变坏；v.t. 使变酸, 使失望；n. 酸味, 酸饮料
